epics.title: Define a concrete draft token baseline for the design rule engine

tasks.title: Audit the current design rule engine assets and songs surface styling cues that should inform the first token baseline
acceptance_criteria: The planning output identifies the existing aesthetic profile, token source, and the primary UI surface that should anchor the baseline.
The task scope distinguishes between stable token values and unresolved decisions that should remain open.
The task does not require product UI edits by itself.
done_definition: The design phase can reference a bounded set of current repo sources instead of inventing a fresh visual direction.

tasks.title: Replace placeholder draft token fields with concrete typography, spacing, color, radius, shadow, and grid baseline values
acceptance_criteria: The design token artifact provides concrete draft values for spacing and typography instead of undecided placeholders.
The token baseline remains aligned with the selected warm restrained editorial utility profile.
Open choices that should remain provisional are recorded as notes rather than omitted.
done_definition: The token file is specific enough to guide future styling work without pretending that every aesthetic choice is finalized.

tasks.title: Bind the token baseline to the current Tailwind-based styling layer and document how builder work should apply it
acceptance_criteria: The design output states how the token baseline maps to the current repo styling layer.
Builder constraints preserve compatibility with the existing Tailwind and CSS variable setup.
The work avoids introducing a second competing token system in the same pass.
done_definition: Implementation can proceed without ambiguity about whether tokens are conceptual only or intended for current repo use.

tasks.title: Update the design token template and any directly related design-rule-engine guidance needed for the new baseline
acceptance_criteria: The token template file is updated with concrete draft values consistent with the approved design output.
Any nearby design-rule-engine guidance that would contradict the new baseline is updated or explicitly left unchanged with rationale.
The edit stays additive and isolated to the design rule engine system unless later workflow steps authorize broader UI adoption.
done_definition: The repository contains a single draft token baseline that matches both the design artifacts and the checked-in spec files.

tasks.title: Validate the updated artifacts and capture any remaining gaps before completion
acceptance_criteria: The updated run artifacts validate against the existing schemas.
Relevant project checks pass for the changed files when applicable.
Remaining draft-level risks are called out explicitly in verification or review output.
done_definition: The run can route through verification and review without hidden unresolved implementation work.

assumptions: The current songs workspace is an acceptable anchor surface for deriving the first warm restrained editorial utility token baseline.
This pass should define draft token values inside the design rule engine rather than migrate the entire app to a dedicated runtime token system.
Geist can remain the immediate type family binding while the hierarchy is primarily refined through size, weight, tracking, and line-height.
risks: If the token baseline chases aesthetic references too aggressively, internal utility surfaces may lose operational clarity and contrast discipline.
If notes and open questions are not preserved, draft values may be mistaken for a finalized design system contract.
Current product surfaces may still use one-off Tailwind values that do not yet align perfectly with the new token baseline.
